items.title: Meeting Minutes
items.description: APPROVE the minutes of the August 19 and 26, 2025, regular City Council meetings

items.title: Proclamation Honoring Bishop Tom H. Watson and Mother Hazel Watson
items.description: PROCLAMATION honoring Bishop Tom H. Watson and Mother Hazel Watson of Gloryland Apostolic Cathedral for over 50 years of ministry and community service

items.title: Contract Amendment No. 3 with Gallagher Benefit Services, Inc.
items.description: APPROVE a third amendment to the contract with Gallagher Benefit Services, Inc. for an additional $50,000 for a total not-to-exceed amount of $150,000, with a term through June 30, 2026, to support employment outreach and recruitment

items.title: Purchase of Hand and Mobile Encrypted Radios from Motorola Solutions, Inc. for the ROCK Program
items.description: APPROVE the purchase of six (6) hand encrypted radios and two (2) mobile radios from Motorola Solutions, in an amount not to exceed $43,406.21 for the Community Crisis Response Program

items.title: Contract with Jumpstart Mastery for Crisis Intervention Training
items.description: APPROVE a contract with Jumpstart Mastery for on-site 3-day training sessions on de-escalation and crisis intervention for an amount not to exceed $41,500

items.title: Reappointment of Andre Jackson to the Community Police Review Commission
items.description: REAPPOINT Andre Jackson to the Community Police Review Commission, with a term that expires on November 1, 2026

items.title: Reappointment of Orrian Willis to the Economic Development Commission
items.description: REAPPOINT Orrian Willis to the Economic Development Commission with a term that expires on March 30, 2028

items.title: Reappointment of Sahai Burrowes to the Community Crisis Response Program Advisory Board
items.description: REAPPOINT Sahai Burrowes to the Community Crisis Response Program Advisory Board with a term that expires on December 31, 2027

items.title: Appointment of Sonia Decker to the Community Crisis Response Program Advisory Board
items.description: APPOINT Sonia Decker to the Community Crisis Response Program Advisory Board with a term that expires on December 31, 2028

items.title: Appointment of Jennifer Koscielniak to the Recreation and Parks Commission
items.description: APPOINT Jennifer Koscielniak to the Recreation and Parks Commission with a term that expires on October 26, 2026

items.title: Establishment of the Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force
items.description: DIRECT the city manager to work with the mayor to establish a Macdonald Avenue Corridor Task Force (a Brown Act body), which is for a limited duration, composed of Mayor Eduardo Martinez, Councilmember Doria Robinson, staff, and stakeholders
items.staff_contact: Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503 / Councilmember Doria Robinson 510-620-6565

items.title: Reappointment of Jason Lindsey to the Workforce Development Board
items.description: REAPPOINT Jason Lindsey to the Workforce Development Board with a term that expires on March 1, 2029

items.title: Interagency Agreement with Contra Costa County - Human Trafficking Operational Support Fund
items.description: ADOPT a resolution and APPROVE the Interagency Agreement with Contra Costa County and ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE the funds received from Contra Costa County to the Police Department's budget for the grant period September 1, 2025, through September 30, 2026

items.title: Accept and Appropriate Grant Funding from CHP Cannabis Tax Fund Grant Program
items.description: ADOPT a resolution to ACCEPT and APPROPRIATE $171,222.58 in grant funding from the California Highway Patrol, Cannabis Tax Fund Grant Program awarded to the Police Department to purchase two (2) DUI enforcement vehicles

items.title: Summary Vacation for a Portion of South 27th Street
items.description: ADOPT a resolution approving the summary vacation of a portion of South 27th Street between Maine Avenue and the southern boundary of Boorman Park

items.title: Purchase Green Seal-Certified Cleaning Products
items.description: APPROVE the purchase of Green Seal-certified cleaning products from Universal Building Services & Supply Co. and Nelson's Building Maintenance, Inc. for three-year terms with two one-year extension options

items.title: Purchase of Three Fleet Vehicles from National Auto Fleet Group
items.description: ADOPT a resolution and APPROVE purchase of one (1) 2026 Freightliner 114SD Plus Lowboy Tractor and two (2) 2026 Freightliner M2106 Plus Dump Trucks

items.title: Cooperative Agreement with Caltrans for Central Avenue I-80 Undercrossing Pedestrian and Bicycle Improvements
items.description: ADOPT a resolution and AUTHORIZE the city manager to execute a cooperative agreement with Caltrans for the City of Richmond's $50,000 contribution to the Central Avenue I-80 Undercrossing Pedestrian and Bicycle Improvements Project

action_items.title: Communication to the public and counseling for families and witnesses of officer involved shootings or an in-custody death
action_items.description: DIRECT the city manager to develop a plan to revise communication protocol after OIS, modify guidelines for released body-cam footage, and issue RFP for counseling services for families and witnesses
action_items.public_comment_note: Public comment was closed at previous meeting - council deliberation and vote only

motions.motion_text: DIRECT the city manager to work with Police Chief French and RPD staff to update existing RPD policy or create new policy to prescribe timing, frequency, and transparency of OIS communications. Includes: 24-hour window for first press release; minimum 30-day frequency of updates; required content for first press release (investigating agencies, investigation sequence, CPRC link, AB 748 body cam info, support services); body cam footage requirements per Gov Code 7923.625; mental health services assessment with recommendations within 6 months; comprehensive plan back to council within 45 days.

friendly_amendments.description: Include other departments regarding the press release policy and keep both versions of body cam policy with both released simultaneously

action_items.title: Amend the Council Rules of Procedures and Order
action_items.description: ADOPT a resolution to amend Council Rules including: reduce meetings from three to two per month; set meeting schedule for first and third Tuesday; Closed Session at 3:30 PM, Open Session at 5:00 PM; meetings adjourn by 9:00 PM with extension option to 10:00 PM by supermajority
action_items.department: City Attorney's Office / City Clerk's Office / City Manager's Office

motions.motion_text: Six-month pilot for three meetings per month with the third meeting for closed session matters only from 4:00 PM to 8:00 PM; first and third council meetings begin at 3:30 PM and end at 5:00 PM; end time of city council meetings would be 10:00 PM with option to extend until 11:00 PM by majority vote

friendly_amendments.description: Hold closed session from 3:30 PM to 5:30 PM
Ensure that the third closed session meeting was a two-hour meeting

motions.motion_text: Reconsider the previous vote (clarification of meeting time requested)

Keep three meetings per month with closed session from 3:30 PM to 6:00 PM; council meeting ending at 10:00 PM with option to extend by supermajority (five votes) until 11:00 PM. Permanent change to rules of procedure (not a pilot); if not working, item would be placed back on agenda for further discussion.

friendly_amendments.description: End at 9:00 PM with option to extend to 10:00 PM by supermajority vote

action_items.title: Presentation from the Contra Costa County Building Trades Council to expand opportunities in Richmond
action_items.description: RECEIVE a presentation from the Contra Costa County Building Trades Council; DIRECT staff to establish a Public Lands Policy to close loopholes, raise job standards, ensure enforceability, and provide career pathways

public_comments_open_forum.summary: Commented on need to fill city vacancies; supported establishment of a task force to assist businesses in the city
Commented on poor conditions of city parks; need to appoint members to Parks and Recreation Commission; need for agreement with WCCUSD for sports field use
Invited community to Josh and Elaina Genser Ribbon Cutting Ceremony at Richmond Police Activities League on Sept 25, 2025
Commented regarding the shooting of Angel Montano
Commented regarding the shooting of Angel Montano
Invited community to El Sobrante Pride 2025 Event on Sept 28 at La Moine (Cheese Park)
Thanked councilmembers and staff for attending community events and building community
Spoke in support of interagency agreement with Contra Costa County for Human Trafficking Operational Support Fund
Thanked those who supported the stop illegal firework campaign
Requested update on $1 million allocated for immigration support fund

written_public_comments.summary: Thanked council for appointment to ROCK community advisory board
Strongly supports Vice Mayor's proposal for Public Lands Policy; emphasizes need for union jobs, permanent affordability, community input, and equitable treatment of all unions

council_reports.summary: Announced East Bay Wildfire Coalition of local governments meeting on Sept 24, 2025
Reported attending groundbreaking of full renovation of Richmond High School, Spirit and Soul Festival, and Eastshore Alliance meeting where Councilmember Robinson received an award
Announced that the puppy he was fostering was adopted
Reported attending Spirit and Soul festival, Richmond Hackathon at CoBiz, and Eastshore Alliance meeting

extraction_notes: Meeting had both Joint SPA session and regular City Council session
P.2 had multiple rounds of voting including a motion to reconsider - coalition dynamics visible
On P.2: Wilson/Bana/Brown/Jimenez formed initial majority (4-3); after reconsideration, Martinez/Robinson/Wilson/Jimenez formed new majority (4-3) with different policy outcome
Bana and Brown shifted from Aye to Nay between original and substitute motions on P.2
Note that city manager confirmed immigration fund and soccer field updates would be provided via email - potential CPRA target for follow-up

P.1_OIS_policy.note: Brown and Zepeda voted Nay on OIS transparency policy - interesting given it was Robinson's motion

P.2_meeting_rules.note: Coalition completely reshuffled between initial and final vote. Jimenez and Wilson were in both winning coalitions. Brown and Bana flipped from Aye to Nay.
